Important Notes for Optimization Part 1

Author: Jiaqing Xie, ETH MSCS

  1. Introdution
  1. Theory of Convex Functions
    1. Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: ∣uTv∣≤∥u∥∥v∥|u^T v| \leq \| u \| \| v \|∣uTv∣≤∥u∥∥v∥﻿,

      sketch proof: define a quadratic function: f(x) = ∑i=1d(uix+vi)2\sum_{i = 1}^{d} (u_i x + v_i)^2∑i=1d​(ui​x+vi​)2﻿ = (∑i=1dui2)x2+(2∑i=1duivi)x+(∑i=1dvi2)( \sum_{i = 1}^d u_i^2 )x^2 + ( 2\sum_{i = 1}^d u_i v_i )x + ( \sum_{i = 1}^d v_i^2 )(∑i=1d​ui2​)x2+(2∑i=1d​ui​vi​)x+(∑i=1d​vi2​)﻿ = ax2+bx+c ax^2 + bx + c ax2+bx+c﻿. We know that f(x)≥0f(x) \geq 0 f(x)≥0﻿ for all x, so b2−4ac≤0b^2 - 4 ac \leq 0b2−4ac≤0﻿. Proof ends.

    1. Spectral norm

      ∥A∥=max⁡v∈Rd,v≠0∥Av∥∥v∥\| A \| = \max_{v \in \mathbb{R}^d, v \neq 0} \frac{\| A v \|}{\| v \|}∥A∥=maxv∈Rd,v=0​∥v∥∥Av∥​﻿,

    1. Mean-value theorem, fundamental of calculus, differentiability

      let h a continuous function in [a, b], then there exists c∈[a,b] c \in [a , b]c∈[a,b]﻿ such that:

      h′(c)=h(b)−h(a)b−ah'(c) = \frac{h(b) - h(a)}{b - a}h′(c)=b−ah(b)−h(a)​﻿

      fundamental of calculus: h(b)−h(a)=∫abh′(t)dth(b) - h(a) = \int_{a}^{b} h'(t)dth(b)−h(a)=∫ab​h′(t)dt﻿

      differentiability: f(y)=f(x)+A(y−x)+r(y−x)f(y) = f(x) + A(y - x) + r(y-x)f(y)=f(x)+A(y−x)+r(y−x)﻿, where lim⁡v→0∥r(v)∥∥v∥=0\lim_{v \rightarrow 0 } \frac{\| r(v) \|}{\| v \|} = 0limv→0​∥v∥∥r(v)∥​=0﻿, AAA﻿ is the Jacobian, where is defined by Df(x)ij=∂fi∂xj(x)Df(x)_{ij} = \frac{\partial f_i}{\partial x_j}(x)Df(x)ij​=∂xj​∂fi​​(x)﻿, then f is called differentiable if f is differentiable at x∈dom(f)x \in dom(f)x∈dom(f)﻿.

      Tangent Hyperplane:f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x) f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y-x)f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿

      Chain Rule: D(f∘g)(x)=Df(g(x))Dg(x)D(f \circ g)(x) = D f(g(x)) D g(x)D(f∘g)(x)=Df(g(x))Dg(x)﻿

    1. Convex Sets

      Defined by any two points x, y in the set C, for all λ∈[0,1] \lambda \in [0, 1]λ∈[0,1]﻿,λx+(1−λ)y∈C \lambda x + (1 - \lambda) y \in Cλx+(1−λ)y∈C﻿

      Observation: CiC_iCi​﻿ be convex sets, Then a combination of CiC_iCi​﻿ is a convex set.

      Two statements are equivalent:

      1) f is B-lipschitz,∥f(x)−f(y)∥≤B∥x−y∥ \| f(x) - f(y) \| \leq B \| x - y \|∥f(x)−f(y)∥≤B∥x−y∥﻿

      2) f has differentials bounded by B: ∥Df(x)∥≤B\| D f(x) \| \leq B∥Df(x)∥≤B﻿

      sketch proof: 1 → 2

      B∥v∥≥∥f(x+v)−f(x)∥=∥Df(x)v+r(v)∥≥∥Df(x)v∥−∥r(v)∥B \| v \| \geq \| f(x+ v) - f(x) \| = \| D f(x) v + r(v) \| \geq \| D f(x) v \| - \| r(v) \|B∥v∥≥∥f(x+v)−f(x)∥=∥Df(x)v+r(v)∥≥∥Df(x)v∥−∥r(v)∥﻿, let v be a unit vector. Then∥Df(x)∥≤B+∥r(v)∥∥v∥ \| D f(x) \| \leq B + \frac{\| r (v) \|}{ \| v \|}∥Df(x)∥≤B+∥v∥∥r(v)∥​﻿. We know that ∥r(v)∥∥v∥→0\frac{\| r (v) \|}{ \| v \|} \rightarrow 0∥v∥∥r(v)∥​→0﻿, therefore ∥Df(x)∥≤B\| D f(x) \| \leq B∥Df(x)∥≤B﻿.

      2 → 1:

      Let h(t)=zTf(x+t(y−x))h(t) = z^T f(x + t(y -x))h(t)=zTf(x+t(y−x))﻿

      h’(t)=zTDf(x+t(y−x))(y−x)h’(t) = z^T D f(x+t(y-x)) (y-x)h’(t)=zTDf(x+t(y−x))(y−x)﻿

      h’(c)=h(1)−h(0)h’(c) = h(1) - h(0)h’(c)=h(1)−h(0)﻿ by mean-value

      ∥zT(f(y)−f(x))∥=∥h(1)−h(0)∥\| z^T ( f(y) - f(x))\| = \| h(1) - h(0) \| ∥zT(f(y)−f(x))∥=∥h(1)−h(0)∥﻿ (By definition of h) = ∥h’(c)∥ \| h’(c) \|∥h’(c)∥﻿

      = zTDf(x+t(y−x))(y−x)z^T D f(x+t(y-x)) (y-x)zTDf(x+t(y−x))(y−x)﻿

      ≤∥z∥∥Df(x+t(y−x))(y−x)∥ \leq \| z \| \| D f(x+t(y-x)) (y-x) \|≤∥z∥∥Df(x+t(y−x))(y−x)∥﻿ ≤B∥z∥∥y−x∥\leq B \|z \| \| y- x\|≤B∥z∥∥y−x∥﻿, so if we set z=f(y)−f(x)∥f(y)−f(x)∥ z = \frac{f(y) - f(x)}{ \| f(y) - f(x)\|}z=∥f(y)−f(x)∥f(y)−f(x)​﻿, then ∥f(x)−f(y)∥≤B∥x−y∥ \| f(x) - f(y) \| \leq B \| x - y \|∥f(x)−f(y)∥≤B∥x−y∥﻿

    1. Convex Functions

      function f is convex if

      1) dom(f) is convex

      2) all x,y∈dom(f) x, y \in dom(f)x,y∈dom(f)﻿, all λ∈[0,1]\lambda \in [0, 1]λ∈[0,1]﻿, we have:f(λx+(1−λ)y)≤λf(x)+(1−λ)f(y): f(\lambda x + (1 - \lambda) y) \leq \lambda f(x) + (1 - \lambda) f(y):f(λx+(1−λ)y)≤λf(x)+(1−λ)f(y)﻿

      epi-graph: epi(f)=(x,α)∈R:x∈dom(f),α≥f(x)epi(f) = {(x, \alpha) \in \mathbb{R}: x \in dom(f), \alpha \geq f(x) }epi(f)=(x,α)∈R:x∈dom(f),α≥f(x)﻿.

      f convex if and only if epi(f) is a convex set, consider (x,α) (x, \alpha)(x,α)﻿ and (y,β) (y, \beta)(y,β)﻿

      sketch proof:

      1) if dom(f) convex: f(λx+(1−λ)y)≤λf(x)+(1−λ)f(y)f(\lambda x + (1 - \lambda) y) \leq \lambda f(x) + (1 - \lambda) f(y)f(λx+(1−λ)y)≤λf(x)+(1−λ)f(y)﻿≤λα+(1−λ)β \leq \lambda \alpha + (1 - \lambda) \beta≤λα+(1−λ)β﻿,

      by definition of epi-graph: λ(x,α)+(1−λ)(y,β)=(λx+(1−λ)y,λα+(1−λ)β)∈epi(f)\lambda (x , \alpha) + (1 - \lambda) (y, \beta) = (\lambda x + (1 - \lambda) y, \lambda \alpha + (1 - \lambda) \beta) \in epi(f)λ(x,α)+(1−λ)(y,β)=(λx+(1−λ)y,λα+(1−λ)β)∈epi(f)﻿

      Jensen’s inequality: f(∑i=1mλixi)≤∑i=1mλif(xi)f(\sum_{i = 1}^{m} \lambda_i x_i) \leq \sum_{i = 1}^{m} \lambda_i f(x_i)f(∑i=1m​λi​xi​)≤∑i=1m​λi​f(xi​)﻿

      let f be convex, dom(f) is open, then f is continuous.

      There exists an vector space V, a linear function f such that f is discontinuous for all v.

      First-order Characteristics: f convex if and only if f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x) f(y) \geq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y - x)f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿

      sketch proof:

      1) if f is convex, then f(x+t(y−x))=f((1−t)x+ty)≤(1−t)f(x)+tf(y)=f(x)+t(f(y)−f(x))f(x + t(y-x)) = f((1 - t)x + ty) \leq (1 - t) f(x) + t f(y) = f(x) + t (f(y) - f(x))f(x+t(y−x))=f((1−t)x+ty)≤(1−t)f(x)+tf(y)=f(x)+t(f(y)−f(x))﻿ ,

      then f(y)≥f(x)+f(x+t(y−x))−f(x)tf(y) \geq f(x) + \frac{f(x + t(y-x)) - f(x)}{t}f(y)≥f(x)+tf(x+t(y−x))−f(x)​﻿

      =f(x)+∇f(x)Tt(y−x)+r(t(y−x))t= f(x) + \frac{\nabla f(x)^T t(y - x) + r(t(y-x))}{t}=f(x)+t∇f(x)Tt(y−x)+r(t(y−x))​﻿

      =f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+r(t(y−x))t= f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y-x) + \frac{r(t(y-x))}{t}=f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+tr(t(y−x))​﻿, since r(t(y−x))t→0\frac{r(t(y-x))}{t} \rightarrow 0tr(t(y−x))​→0﻿,f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x) f(y) \geq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y-x)f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿.

      2) if we already know f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x) f(y) \geq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y - x)f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿, then we could let z=λx+(1−λ)yz = \lambda x + (1 - \lambda) yz=λx+(1−λ)y﻿, then

      f(x)≥f(z)+∇f(z)T(x−z)f(x) \geq f(z) + \nabla f(z)^T (x - z)f(x)≥f(z)+∇f(z)T(x−z)﻿ andf(y)≥f(z)+∇f(z)T(y−z) f(y) \geq f(z) + \nabla f(z)^T (y - z)f(y)≥f(z)+∇f(z)T(y−z)﻿

      λf(x)+(1−λ)f(y)≥λf(z)+λ∇f(z)T(x−z)+(1−λ)f(z)+(1−λ)∇f(z)T(y−z)\lambda f(x) + (1 - \lambda) f(y) \geq \lambda f(z) + \lambda \nabla f(z)^T (x - z) + ( 1 - \lambda) f(z) + (1 - \lambda )\nabla f(z)^T (y - z)λf(x)+(1−λ)f(y)≥λf(z)+λ∇f(z)T(x−z)+(1−λ)f(z)+(1−λ)∇f(z)T(y−z)﻿ =f(z)= f(z)=f(z)﻿ =f(λx+(1−λ)y)= f(\lambda x + (1 - \lambda) y)=f(λx+(1−λ)y)﻿. proof ends.

      f is if and only if dom(f) is convex and (∇f(y)−∇f(x))T(y−x)≥0(\nabla f(y) - \nabla f(x))^T ( y- x) \geq 0(∇f(y)−∇f(x))T(y−x)≥0﻿

      Sketch proof: if f is convex, then

      f(x)≥f(y)+∇f(y)T(x−y)f(x) \geq f(y) + \nabla f(y)^T (x - y)f(x)≥f(y)+∇f(y)T(x−y)﻿ andf(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x) f(y) \geq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y - x)f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿, add together and multiplied by -1 yields the answer.

      Other direction: (∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T(t(y−x))≥0↔(∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T(y−x)≥0(\nabla f(x + t(y-x)) - \nabla f(x))^T ( t(y- x)) \geq 0 \leftrightarrow (\nabla f(x + t(y-x)) - \nabla f(x))^T (y- x) \geq 0 (∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T(t(y−x))≥0↔(∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T(y−x)≥0﻿. Leth=f(x+t(y−x)) h = f(x+ t(y-x))h=f(x+t(y−x))﻿, then h′(t)=(∇f(x+t(y−x))T(y−x)h'(t) = (\nabla f(x + t(y-x))^T ( y- x)h′(t)=(∇f(x+t(y−x))T(y−x)﻿. Thereforeh′(t)≥∇f(x)T(y−x) h'(t) \geq \nabla f(x)^T (y-x)h′(t)≥∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿. from mean-value theorem, we know h’(c)=h(1)−h(0),c∈[0,1]h’(c) = h(1) - h(0), c \in [0, 1]h’(c)=h(1)−h(0),c∈[0,1]﻿.

      f(y)=h(1)=h(0)+h′(c)=f(x)+h′(c)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)f(y) = h(1) = h(0) + h'(c) = f(x) + h'(c) \geq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y-x)f(y)=h(1)=h(0)+h′(c)=f(x)+h′(c)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿

      Second-order Characteristics: the Hessian is positive semidefinite

      Operation perserves convexity: max, sum

      2) let f be convex, let g=Ax+bg = Ax + bg=Ax+b﻿, then f(g(x))=f(Ax+b)f(g(x)) = f(Ax+ b)f(g(x))=f(Ax+b)﻿ is convex on dom(f∘g f \circ gf∘g﻿)

    1. Minimizing Convex Functions

      x∗x^*x∗﻿ local minimizer on f, then x∗x^*x∗﻿ is a global minimizer.

      sketch proof: proof by contradiction, suppose f(y)<f(x∗)f(y) < f(x^*)f(y)<f(x∗)﻿. Define y’=λx∗+(1−λ)yy’ = \lambda x^* + (1 - \lambda ) yy’=λx∗+(1−λ)y﻿, by convexity, we have f(y’)<f(x∗)f(y’) < f(x^*)f(y’)<f(x∗)﻿. chooseλ \lambda λ﻿ close to 1, yield to a contradiction.

      If ∇f(x)\nabla f(x) ∇f(x)﻿= 0, then x is a global minimum. proof: first-order characteristics

      The reverse stands but it didnt require convexity.

      Strictly Convex, no equality for first-order and second order. and only one global minimum exists.

      Optimal condition: x∗ x^*x∗﻿ is a minimizer if ∇f(x∗)(x−x∗)≥0\nabla f(x^*) (x - x^*) \geq 0∇f(x∗)(x−x∗)≥0﻿

    1. Existence of Minimizer

      Sub-level sets: f≤α={x∈Rd:f(x)≤α}f^{\leq \alpha} = \{ x \in \mathbb{R}^d : f(x) \leq \alpha \}f≤α={x∈Rd:f(x)≤α}﻿ is a sublevel set of f. continuous and closed.

      Nonempty and bounded sublevel set f≤α f^{\leq \alpha}f≤α﻿, then f has a global minimum.

      If f≤αf^{\leq \alpha}f≤α﻿ doesnt exist, then it may or may not have a global minimum.

      Recession Cone

    1. Convex Programming

      an optimization problem in standard form:

      min⁡f0(x)\min_{f_0(x)}minf0​(x)​﻿,

      subject to: fi(x)≤0f_i(x) \leq 0fi​(x)≤0﻿, hi(x)=0h_i(x) = 0hi​(x)=0﻿

      Lagrangian: L(x,λ,ν)=f0(x)+∑i=1mλifi(x)+∑i=1pνihi(x)L(x, \lambda, \nu) = f_0(x) + \sum_{i = 1}^{m} \lambda_i f_i(x) + \sum_{i = 1}^{p} \nu_i h_i(x) L(x,λ,ν)=f0​(x)+∑i=1m​λi​fi​(x)+∑i=1p​νi​hi​(x)﻿

      Lagrange dual function: g(λ,ν)=inf⁡x∈DL(x,λ,ν)g(\lambda, \nu) = \inf_{x \in \mathcal{D}} L(x, \lambda, \nu)g(λ,ν)=infx∈D​L(x,λ,ν)﻿

      Weak Duality: g(λ,ν)≤f0(x)g(\lambda, \nu) \leq f_0(x)g(λ,ν)≤f0​(x)﻿ (easily observed)

      Problem becomes:

      max⁡g(λ,ν)\max g(\lambda, \nu)maxg(λ,ν)﻿

      s.t.λ≥0s.t. \lambda \geq 0s.t.λ≥0﻿

      Convex Program ===⇒ min⁡g(λ,ν),λ≥0\min g(\lambda, \nu), \lambda \geq 0ming(λ,ν),λ≥0﻿

      Suppose a feasible solution x^\hat{x}x^﻿ which satisfied fi(x^)<0 f_i(\hat{x}) < 0fi​(x^)<0﻿, then infimum of f0(x)f_0(x)f0​(x)﻿ equals the supremum of g(λ,ν)g(\lambda, \nu) g(λ,ν)﻿.

      zero duality gap: g(λ,ν)≤f0(x)g(\lambda, \nu) \leq f_0(x)g(λ,ν)≤f0​(x)﻿

      Complementary slackness: λifi(x)=0\lambda_i f_i(x) = 0λi​fi​(x)=0﻿

      KKT cocndition: λifi(x)=0\lambda_i f_i(x) = 0λi​fi​(x)=0﻿, ∇(L(x,λ,ν))=∇(f0(x)+∑i=1mλifi(x)+∑i=1pνihi(x))=0\nabla (L(x, \lambda, \nu)) = \nabla( f_0(x) + \sum_{i = 1}^{m} \lambda_i f_i(x) + \sum_{i = 1}^{p} \nu_i h_i(x)) = 0 ∇(L(x,λ,ν))=∇(f0​(x)+∑i=1m​λi​fi​(x)+∑i=1p​νi​hi​(x))=0﻿

  1. Gradient Descent
    1. Let f be convex, x∗x^*x∗﻿ be the global minimum, then the goal is to find f(xT)−f(x∗)<ϵf(x_T) - f(x^*) < \epsilonf(xT​)−f(x∗)<ϵ﻿ in some iteration T.
      Lipschitz-ConvexSmooth-ConvexStrongly-Convex Smooth & Strongly- Convex
      GDO(1/ϵ2)O(1/\epsilon^2)O(1/ϵ2)﻿O(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿O(1/log⁡(ϵ))O(1/\log(\epsilon))O(1/log(ϵ))﻿
      AGDO(1/ϵ)O(1/\sqrt{\epsilon})O(1/ϵ​)﻿
      PGDO(1/ϵ2)O(1/\epsilon^2)O(1/ϵ2)﻿O(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿O(1/log⁡(ϵ))O(1/\log(\epsilon))O(1/log(ϵ))﻿
      SubgradientO(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿O(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿
      SGDO(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿O(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿
    1. Algorithm:
      1. xt+1=xt+vtx_{t+1} = x_t + v_txt+1​=xt​+vt​﻿, where vtv_t vt​﻿ is the step at time t.
      1. f(xt+vt)=f(xt)+∇f(xt)Tvt+r(vt)≈f(xt)+∇f(xt)Tvtf(x_t + v_t) = f(x_t) + \nabla f(x_t)^T v_t + r(v_t) \approx f(x_t) + \nabla f(x_t)^T v_tf(xt​+vt​)=f(xt​)+∇f(xt​)Tvt​+r(vt​)≈f(xt​)+∇f(xt​)Tvt​﻿
      1. GD defined by: xt+1=xt−γ∇f(xt)x_{t+1} = x_t - \gamma \nabla f(x_t)xt+1​=xt​−γ∇f(xt​)﻿
    1. Vanilla Analysis:
      1. First-order: f(x∗)−f(xt)≥∇f(xt)T(x∗−xt)→f(xt)−f(x∗)≤∇f(xt)T(xt−x∗) f(x^*) - f(x_t) \geq \nabla f(x_t)^T (x^* - x_t) \rightarrow f(x_t) - f(x^*) \leq \nabla f(x_t)^T (x_t - x^*) f(x∗)−f(xt​)≥∇f(xt​)T(x∗−xt​)→f(xt​)−f(x∗)≤∇f(xt​)T(xt​−x∗)﻿, let gt=∇f(xt) g_t = \nabla f(x_t) gt​=∇f(xt​)﻿, then by the definition of GD, gt=xt−xt+1γg_t = \frac{x_t - x_{t+1}}{\gamma}gt​=γxt​−xt+1​​﻿.
      1. Then,∇f(xt)T(xt−x∗)=gtT(xt−x∗)=(xt−xt+1)Tγ(xt−x∗) \nabla f(x_t)^T (x_t - x^*) = g_t^T (x_t - x^*) = \frac{(x_t - x_{t+1})^T}{\gamma}(x_t - x^*)∇f(xt​)T(xt​−x∗)=gtT​(xt​−x∗)=γ(xt​−xt+1​)T​(xt​−x∗)﻿.
      1. We know that for two vectors a and b, 2aTb=∥a∥2+∥b∥2−∥a−b∥22 a^T b = \| a\|^2 + \|b \|^2 - \| a - b \|^22aTb=∥a∥2+∥b∥2−∥a−b∥2﻿
      1. (xt−xt+1)Tγ(xt−x∗)=12γ(∥xt−xt+1∥2+∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2) \frac{(x_t - x_{t+1})^T}{\gamma}(x_t - x^*) = \frac{1}{2\gamma}(\| x_t - x_{t+1} \|^2 + \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - x_{t+1} \|^2)γ(xt​−xt+1​)T​(xt​−x∗)=2γ1​(∥xt​−xt+1​∥2+∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2)﻿

        =12γ(γ2∥gt∥2+∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2)= \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \gamma^2\| g_t \|^2 + \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - x_{t+1} \|^2)=2γ1​(γ2∥gt​∥2+∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2)﻿

        =γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2)=\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - x_{t+1} \|^2)=2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2)﻿

        Telescoping:

        ∑t=0T−1gtT(xt−x∗)=∑t=0T−1γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2)\sum_{t = 0}^{T -1 } g_t^T (x_t - x^*) = \sum_{t = 0}^{T-1}\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - x_{t+1} \|^2)∑t=0T−1​gtT​(xt​−x∗)=∑t=0T−1​2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2)﻿

        ≤∑t=0T−1γ2∥gt∥2+12γ∥x0−x∗∥2\leq \sum_{t = 0}^{T-1}\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2 \gamma} \|x_0 - x^* \|^2≤∑t=0T−1​2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿

        Therefore the average error for f(xt)−f(x∗)f(x_t) - f(x^*)f(xt​)−f(x∗)﻿ is: ∑t=0T−1γ2∥gt∥2+12γ∥x0−x∗∥2 \sum_{t = 0}^{T-1}\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2 \gamma} \|x_0 - x^* \|^2∑t=0T−1​2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿

    1. Lipschitz-Convex:

      suppose ∥x0−x∗∥≤R\| x_0 - x^* \| \leq R∥x0​−x∗∥≤R﻿, and ∥∇f(x)∥≤B\| \nabla f(x) \| \leq B ∥∇f(x)∥≤B﻿ for all x, then choose the step size γ=RBT\gamma = \frac{R}{B \sqrt{T}}γ=BT​R​﻿ yields 1T∑t=0T−1(f(xt)−f∗)≤RBT\frac{1}{T} \sum_{t = 0}^{T-1} ( f(x_t) - f^* ) \leq \frac{RB}{\sqrt{T}}T1​∑t=0T−1​(f(xt​)−f∗)≤T​RB​﻿. Sketch proof: just plugin the conditions to the above inequality. ⇒ O(1/ϵ2)O(1/\epsilon^2)O(1/ϵ2)﻿

    1. Smooth-Convex:

      L-smooth: f(y)≤f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+L2∥y−x∥2f(y) \leq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y - x) + \frac{L}{2} \| y - x \|^ 2f(y)≤f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+2L​∥y−x∥2﻿

      L-smooth ↔\leftrightarrow↔﻿ ∥∇f(x)−∇f(y)∥≤L∥x−y∥\| \nabla f(x) - \nabla f(y) \| \leq L \| x - y \|∥∇f(x)−∇f(y)∥≤L∥x−y∥﻿

      Sufficient decrease: γ=1L\gamma = \frac{1}{L}γ=L1​﻿, then f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−12L∥∇f(xt)∥2f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−2L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2﻿

      Sketch proof: 1) xt+1−xt=−∇f(xt)/Lx_{t+1} - x_t = - \nabla f(x_t) / Lxt+1​−xt​=−∇f(xt​)/L﻿

      2) take this into L-smooth definition

      suppose γ=1L\gamma = \frac{1}{L}γ=L1​﻿, then we perform telescoping: 12L∑t=0T−1∥∇f(xt)∥2≤f(x0)−f(xT)\frac{1}{2L} \sum_{t = 0}^{T-1} \| \nabla f(x_t)\|^2 \leq f(x_0) - f(x_T)2L1​∑t=0T−1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2≤f(x0​)−f(xT​)﻿

      We have proved ∑t=0T−1(f(xt)−f(x∗))≤\sum_{t = 0}^{T-1} (f(x_t) - f(x^*)) \leq∑t=0T−1​(f(xt​)−f(x∗))≤﻿ ∑t=0T−1γ2∥gt∥2+12γ∥x0−x∗∥2 \sum_{t = 0}^{T-1}\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2 \gamma} \|x_0 - x^* \|^2∑t=0T−1​2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿, then substitute γ=1L\gamma = \frac{1}{L}γ=L1​﻿, we’ll have:∑t=0T−112L∥gt∥2+L2∥x0−x∗∥2≤f(x0)−f(xT)+L2∥x0−x∗∥2\sum_{t = 0}^{T-1}\frac{1}{2L}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{L}{2} \|x_0 - x^* \|^2 \leq f(x_0) - f(x_T) + \frac{L}{2} \|x_0 - x^* \|^2∑t=0T−1​2L1​∥gt​∥2+2L​∥x0​−x∗∥2≤f(x0​)−f(xT​)+2L​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿.

      Therefore:∑t=1T−1(f(xt)−f(x∗))≤L2∥x0−x∗∥2 \sum_{t = 1}^{T-1} (f(x_t) - f(x^*)) \leq \frac{L}{2} \|x_0 - x^* \|^2∑t=1T−1​(f(xt​)−f(x∗))≤2L​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿ (t Starts from 1)

      By taking the average: f(xT)−f(x∗)≤1T∑t=1T−1(f(xt)−f(x∗))≤L2T∥x0−x∗∥2f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{1}{T} \sum_{t = 1}^{T-1} (f(x_t) - f(x^*)) \leq \frac{L}{2T} \|x_0 - x^* \|^2f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤T1​∑t=1T−1​(f(xt​)−f(x∗))≤2TL​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿ ⇒ O(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿

    1. Acceleration: (for smooth convex)

      algo: yt+1=xt−1L∇f(xt)y_{t+1} = x_t - \frac{1}{L} \nabla f(x_t)yt+1​=xt​−L1​∇f(xt​)﻿, zt+1=zt−t+12L∇f(xt)z_{t+1} = z_t - \frac{t+1}{2L} \nabla f(x_t)zt+1​=zt​−2Lt+1​∇f(xt​)﻿, xt+1=t+1t+3yt+1+2t+3zt+1x_{t+1} = \frac{t+1}{t+3} y_{t+1} + \frac{2}{t+3} z_{t+1}xt+1​=t+3t+1​yt+1​+t+32​zt+1​﻿,

      it yields:

      f(yT)−f(x∗)≤2L∥z0−x∗∥T(T+1)f(y_T) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{2L \| z_0 - x^* \|}{T(T+1)}f(yT​)−f(x∗)≤T(T+1)2L∥z0​−x∗∥​﻿

      Proof:

      potential function argument: Φ(t)=t(t+1)(f(yt)−f∗)+2L∥zt−x∗∥2\Phi(t) = t(t+1)(f(y_t) - f^*) + 2L \|z_t - x^* \|^2Φ(t)=t(t+1)(f(yt​)−f∗)+2L∥zt​−x∗∥2﻿, non-increasing. If we show its non-increasing, then Φ(T)≤Φ(0)\Phi(T) \leq \Phi(0) Φ(T)≤Φ(0)﻿, proves the theorem

      1. f(yt+1)≤f(xt)−12L∥∇f(xt)∥2f(y_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2f(yt+1​)≤f(xt​)−2L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2﻿
      1. use the vanilla analysis with γ=t+12L\gamma = \frac{t+1}{2L}γ=2Lt+1​﻿, gt=∇f(xt)g_t = \nabla f(x_t)gt​=∇f(xt​)﻿

      gtT(zt−x∗)=t+14L∥gt∥2+Lt+1(∥zt−x∥2−∥x∗−zt+1∥2)g_t^T(z_t - x^*)=\frac{t+1}{4L}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{L}{t+1}( \| z_t - x^{}\|^2 - \|x^* - z_{t+1} \|^2)gtT​(zt​−x∗)=4Lt+1​∥gt​∥2+t+1L​(∥zt​−x∥2−∥x∗−zt+1​∥2)﻿

      1. convexity f(xt)−f(w)≤gtT(xt−w)f(x_t) - f(w) \leq g_t^T (x_t - w)f(xt​)−f(w)≤gtT​(xt​−w)﻿
      1. LetΔ=Φ(t+1)−Φ(t)t+1\Delta = \frac{\Phi(t+1) - \Phi(t)}{t+1}Δ=t+1Φ(t+1)−Φ(t)​﻿

      Then Δ=t(f(yt+1)−f(yt))+2(f(yt+1)−f(x∗))+2Lt+1(∥zt+1−x∗∥2−∥zt−x∗∥2)\Delta = t(f(y_{t+1}) - f(y_t)) + 2(f(y_{t+1}) - f(x^*)) + \frac{2L}{t+1}(\| z_{t+1} - x^* \|^2 - \| z_t - x^* \|^2 )Δ=t(f(yt+1​)−f(yt​))+2(f(yt+1​)−f(x∗))+t+12L​(∥zt+1​−x∗∥2−∥zt​−x∗∥2)﻿

      =t(f(yt+1)−f(yt))+2(f(yt+1)−f(x∗))+t+12L∥gt∥2−2gtT(zt−x∗)= t(f(y_{t+1}) - f(y_t)) + 2(f(y_{t+1}) - f(x^*)) + \frac{t+1}{2L} \| g_t\|^2 - 2g_t^T(z_t - x^*)=t(f(yt+1​)−f(yt​))+2(f(yt+1​)−f(x∗))+2Lt+1​∥gt​∥2−2gtT​(zt​−x∗)﻿

      ≤t(f(xt)−f(yt))+2(f(xt)−f(x∗))−12L∥gt∥2−2gtT(zt−x∗)\leq t(f(x_{t}) - f(y_t)) + 2(f(x_t) - f(x^*)) - \frac{1}{2L} \| g_t\|^2 - 2g_t^T(z_t - x^*)≤t(f(xt​)−f(yt​))+2(f(xt​)−f(x∗))−2L1​∥gt​∥2−2gtT​(zt​−x∗)﻿

      ≤t(f(xt)−f(yt))+2(f(xt)−f(x∗))−2gtT(zt−x∗)\leq t(f(x_{t}) - f(y_t)) + 2(f(x_t) - f(x^*)) - 2g_t^T(z_t - x^*)≤t(f(xt​)−f(yt​))+2(f(xt​)−f(x∗))−2gtT​(zt​−x∗)﻿

      ≤tgtT(xt−yt)+2gtT(xt−x∗)−2gtT(zt−x∗)\leq t g_t^T (x_t - y_t) + 2 g_t^T(x_t - x^*) - 2g_t^T(z_t - x^*)≤tgtT​(xt​−yt​)+2gtT​(xt​−x∗)−2gtT​(zt​−x∗)﻿

      =gtT((t+2)xt−tyt−2zt)= g_t^T ((t+2)x_t - ty_t - 2z_t)=gtT​((t+2)xt​−tyt​−2zt​)﻿ = 0 ⇒ non-increasing ⇒ O(1/ϵ)O(1/\sqrt{\epsilon}) O(1/ϵ​)﻿

    1. Smooth & Strongly- Convex:

      μ\muμ﻿-strongly-convex: f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+μ2∥y−x∥2f(y) \geq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y - x) + \frac{\mu}{2} \| y - x \|^2f(y)≥f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+2μ​∥y−x∥2﻿

      L-smooth: f(y)≤f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+L2∥y−x∥2f(y) \leq f(x) + \nabla f(x)^T (y - x) + \frac{L}{2} \| y - x \|^2f(y)≤f(x)+∇f(x)T(y−x)+2L​∥y−x∥2﻿

      Therefore, ∇f(xt)T(xt−x∗)≥f(xt)−f(x∗)+μ2∥xt−x∗∥2\nabla f(x_t)^T (x_t - x^*) \geq f(x_t) - f(x^*) + \frac{\mu}{2} \| x_t - x^* \|^2∇f(xt​)T(xt​−x∗)≥f(xt​)−f(x∗)+2μ​∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      From the vanilla analysis we have:

      f(xt)−f(x∗)≤12γ(γ2∥∇f(xt)∥2+∥xt−x∗∥2−∥xt+1−x∗∥2)−μ2∥xt−x∗∥2f(x_t) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{1}{2 \gamma} (\gamma^2 \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2 + \| x_t - x^*\|^2 - \|x_{t+1} - x^*\|^2) - \frac{\mu}{2} \| x_t - x^* \|^2f(xt​)−f(x∗)≤2γ1​(γ2∥∇f(xt​)∥2+∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥xt+1​−x∗∥2)−2μ​∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      ⇒ ∥xt+1−x∗∥2≤2γ(f(x∗)−f(xt))+γ2∥∇f(xt)∥2+(1−μγ)∥xt−x∗∥2 \|x_{t+1} - x^*\|^2 \leq 2 \gamma (f(x^*) - f(x_t)) + \gamma^2 \| \nabla f(x_t)\|^2 + (1 - \mu \gamma) \| x_t - x^* \|^2∥xt+1​−x∗∥2≤2γ(f(x∗)−f(xt​))+γ2∥∇f(xt​)∥2+(1−μγ)∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      We choose γ=1L\gamma = \frac{1}{L}γ=L1​﻿, then

      1) ∥xt+1−x∗∥2≤(1−μL)∥xt−x∗∥2\| x_{t+1} - x^* \|^2 \leq (1 - \frac{\mu}{L})\| x_t - x^* \|^2∥xt+1​−x∗∥2≤(1−Lμ​)∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      sketch proof: f(x∗)−f(xt)≤f(xt+1)−f(xt)≤−12L∥∇f(xt)∥2f(x^*) - f(x_t) \leq f(x_{t+1}) - f(x_t) \leq - \frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2f(x∗)−f(xt​)≤f(xt+1​)−f(xt​)≤−2L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2﻿, so 2γ(f(x∗)−f(xt))+γ2∥∇f(xt)∥2≤0 2\gamma (f(x^*) - f(x_t)) + \gamma^2 \| \nabla f(x_t)\|^2 \leq 02γ(f(x∗)−f(xt​))+γ2∥∇f(xt​)∥2≤0﻿ ⇒ ∥xt+1−x∗∥2≤(1−μL)∥xt−x∗∥2 \|x_{t+1} - x^*\|^2 \leq (1 - \frac{\mu}{L})\|x_t - x^*\|^2∥xt+1​−x∗∥2≤(1−Lμ​)∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      2) f(xT)−f(x∗)≤L2(1−μL)T∥x0−x∗∥2f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{L}{2} (1 - \frac{\mu}{L})^T \| x_0 - x^*\|^2 f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤2L​(1−Lμ​)T∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿

      sketch proof:f(xT)−f(x∗)≤∇f(x∗)T(xT−x∗)+L2∥xT−x∗∥=L2∥xT−x∗∥ f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \nabla f(x^*)^T(x_T - x^*) + \frac{L}{2} \| x_T - x^*\| = \frac{L}{2} \| x_T - x^*\|f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤∇f(x∗)T(xT​−x∗)+2L​∥xT​−x∗∥=2L​∥xT​−x∗∥﻿, substitute 1) into this inequality results the answer.

  1. Projected Gradient Descent
    1. algo

      yt+1=xt−γ∇f(xt)y_{t+1} = x_t - \gamma \nabla f(x_t)yt+1​=xt​−γ∇f(xt​)﻿

      xt+1=ΠX(yt+1)=arg min⁡x∈X∥x−yt+1∥2x_{t+1} = \Pi_X (y_{t+1}) = \argmin_{x \in X} \| x - y_{t+1}\|^2xt+1​=ΠX​(yt+1​)=argminx∈X​∥x−yt+1​∥2﻿

      Properties:

      (i) (x−ΠX(y))T(y−ΠX(y))≤0(x - \Pi_X(y))^T(y - \Pi_X(y)) \leq 0(x−ΠX​(y))T(y−ΠX​(y))≤0﻿

      (ii) ∥x−ΠX(y)∥2+∥y−ΠX(y)∥2≤∥x−y∥2\| x - \Pi_X(y) \|^2 + \| y - \Pi_X(y) \|^2 \leq \| x - y \|^2∥x−ΠX​(y)∥2+∥y−ΠX​(y)∥2≤∥x−y∥2﻿

    1. bounded gradients O(1/ϵ2)O(1/\epsilon^2)O(1/ϵ2)﻿

      Vanilla Analysis but with xt+1=yt+1x_{t+1} = y_{t+1}xt+1​=yt+1​﻿:

      gtT(xt−x∗)=γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−yt+1∥2)g_t^T(x_t - x^*)=\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - y_{t+1} \|^2)gtT​(xt​−x∗)=2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−yt+1​∥2)﻿

      ∥xt+1−x∗∥2≤∥x∗−yt+1∥2\| x_{t+1} - x^* \|^2 \leq \|x^* - y_{t+1} \|^2 ∥xt+1​−x∗∥2≤∥x∗−yt+1​∥2﻿

      ⇒ gtT(xt−x∗)≤γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2)g_t^T(x_t - x^*) \leq \frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - x_{t+1} \|^2)gtT​(xt​−x∗)≤2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2)﻿, then same as GD

    1. smooth convex O(1/ϵ)O(1/\epsilon)O(1/ϵ)﻿

      f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−12L∥∇f(xt)∥2+L2∥yt+1−xt+1∥2f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2 + \frac{L}{2} \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1}\|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−2L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2+2L​∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2﻿

      proof:

      start with definition of L-smooth:

      f(xt+1)≤f(xt)+∇f(xt)T(xt+1−xt)+L2∥xt+1−xt∥2f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) + \nabla f(x_t)^T (x_{t+1} - x_t) + \frac{L}{2} \| x_{t+1} - x_t \|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)+∇f(xt​)T(xt+1​−xt​)+2L​∥xt+1​−xt​∥2﻿

      and yt+1=xt−∇f(xt)/Ly_{t+1} = x_t - \nabla f(x_t) /Lyt+1​=xt​−∇f(xt​)/L﻿

      ⇒ f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−L(yt+1−xt)T(xt+1−xt)+L2∥xt+1−xt∥2f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - L (y_{t+1} - x_t)^T (x_{t+1} - x_t) + \frac{L}{2} \| x_{t+1} - x_t \|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−L(yt+1​−xt​)T(xt+1​−xt​)+2L​∥xt+1​−xt​∥2﻿

      =f(xt)−L2(∥yt+1−xt∥2+∥xt+1−xt∥2−∥yt+1−xt+1∥2)+L2∥xt+1−xt∥2 = f(x_t) - \frac{L}{2}(\| y_{t+1} - x_t \|^2 + \| x_{t+1} - x_t \|^2 - \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2) + \frac{L}{2} \| x_{t+1} - x_t \|^2=f(xt​)−2L​(∥yt+1​−xt​∥2+∥xt+1​−xt​∥2−∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2)+2L​∥xt+1​−xt​∥2﻿

      =f(xt)−12L∥∇f(xt)∥2+L2∥yt+1−xt+1∥2= f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2 + \frac{L}{2} \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1}\|^2=f(xt​)−2L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2+2L​∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2﻿

      f(xT)−f(x∗)≤L2T∥x0−x∗∥2f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{L}{2T} \| x_0 - x^*\|^2f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤2TL​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿

      prove:

      from the above inequality: 12L∥∇f(xt)∥2≤f(xt)−f(xt+1)+L2∥yt+1−xt+1∥2\frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2 \leq f(x_t) - f(x_{t+1}) +\frac{L}{2} \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1}\|^22L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2≤f(xt​)−f(xt+1​)+2L​∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2﻿

      Therefore 12L∑t=0T−1∥gt∥2≤L2∑t=0T−1∥yt+1−xt+1∥2+f(x0)−f(xT)\frac{1}{2L}\sum_{t=0}^{T-1} \| g_t\|^2 \leq \frac{L}{2} \sum_{t=0}^{T-1} \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2 + f(x_0) - f(x_T)2L1​∑t=0T−1​∥gt​∥2≤2L​∑t=0T−1​∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2+f(x0​)−f(xT​)﻿

      gtT(xt−x∗)=γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−yt+1∥2)g_t^T(x_t - x^{*})=\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - y_{t+1} \|^2)gtT​(xt​−x∗)=2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−yt+1​∥2)﻿

      upper bounded by: γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2−∥yt+1−xt+1∥2)\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - x_{t+1} \|^2 - \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2)2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2−∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2)﻿

      from first-order characteristics:

      ∑t=0T−1(f(xt)−f(x∗))≤∑t=0T−1gtT(xt−x∗)≤∑t=0T−1γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2−∥yt+1−xt+1∥2)≤12L∑t=0T−1∥gt∥2+L2∥x0−x∗∥2−L2∑t=0T−1∥yt+1−xt+1∥2≤f(x0)−f(xT)+L2∥x0−x∗∥2\sum_{t = 0}^{T -1} (f(x_t) - f(x^*)) \leq \sum_{t = 0}^{T -1} g_t^T (x_t - x^*) \leq \sum_{t = 0}^{T -1} \frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^* - x_{t+1} \|^2 - \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2) \leq \frac{1}{2L}\sum_{t = 0}^{T -1} \| g_t \|^2 + \frac{L}{2} \| x_0 - x^* \|^2 - \frac{L}{2}\sum_{t = 0}^{T -1}\| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2 \leq f(x_0) - f(x_T) + \frac{L}{2} \| x_0 - x^* \|^2∑t=0T−1​(f(xt​)−f(x∗))≤∑t=0T−1​gtT​(xt​−x∗)≤∑t=0T−1​2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2−∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2)≤2L1​∑t=0T−1​∥gt​∥2+2L​∥x0​−x∗∥2−2L​∑t=0T−1​∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2≤f(x0​)−f(xT​)+2L​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿

      Therefore f(xT)−f(x∗)≤L2T∥x0−x∗∥2f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{L}{2T} \| x_0 - x^*\|^2f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤2TL​∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿

    1. smooth and strongly convex O(log(1/ϵ))O(log(1/\epsilon))O(log(1/ϵ))﻿

      ∥xt+1−x∗∥2≤(1−μL)∥xt−x∗∥2\| x_{t+1} - x^* \|^2 \leq (1 - \frac{\mu}{L}) \| x_t - x^*\|^2∥xt+1​−x∗∥2≤(1−Lμ​)∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      Sketch Proof: use the above γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2−∥yt+1−xt+1∥2)\frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^{*} - x_{t+1} \|^2 - \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2)2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2−∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2)﻿, and the strongly-convex function.

      f(xt)−f(x∗)≤γ2∥gt∥2+12γ(∥xt−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1∥2−∥yt+1−xt+1∥2)−μ2∥xt−x∗∥2f(x_t) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{\gamma}{2}\| g_t \|^2 + \frac{1}{2\gamma}( \| x_t - x^{*}\|^2 - \|x^{*} - x_{t+1} \|^2 - \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2) - \frac{\mu}{2} \| x_t - x^* \|^2f(xt​)−f(x∗)≤2γ​∥gt​∥2+2γ1​(∥xt​−x∗∥2−∥x∗−xt+1​∥2−∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2)−2μ​∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      ⇒ ∥xt+1−x∗∥2≤2γ(f(xt)−f(x∗))+γ2∥∇f(xt)∥2−∥yt+1−xt+1∥2+(1−μγ)∥xt−x∗∥2≤(1−μγ)∥xt−x∗∥2\| x_{t+1} - x^* \|^2 \leq 2\gamma (f(x_t) - f(x^*)) + \gamma^2 \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2 - \| y_{t+1} - x_{t+1} \|^2 + (1 - \mu \gamma) \| x_t - x^* \|^2 \leq (1 - \mu \gamma) \| x_t - x^* \|^2∥xt+1​−x∗∥2≤2γ(f(xt​)−f(x∗))+γ2∥∇f(xt​)∥2−∥yt+1​−xt+1​∥2+(1−μγ)∥xt​−x∗∥2≤(1−μγ)∥xt​−x∗∥2﻿

      f(xT)−f(x∗)≤∥∇f(x∗)∥(1−μL)T/2∥x0−x∗∥+L2(1−μL)T∥x0−x∗∥2f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \| \nabla f(x^*) \| (1 - \frac{\mu}{L})^{T/2} \| x_0 - x^* \| + \frac{L}{2} (1 - \frac{\mu}{L})^T \| x_0 - x^* \|^2f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤∥∇f(x∗)∥(1−Lμ​)T/2∥x0​−x∗∥+2L​(1−Lμ​)T∥x0​−x∗∥2﻿

      proof:

      f(xT)−f(x∗)≤∇f(x∗)(xT−x∗)+L2∥x∗−xT∥2f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \nabla f(x^*)(x_T - x^*) + \frac{L}{2} \| x^* - x_T \|^2f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤∇f(x∗)(xT​−x∗)+2L​∥x∗−xT​∥2﻿

      ≤∥∇f(x∗)∥∥xT−x∗∥+L2∥x∗−xT∥2 \leq \| \nabla f(x^*)\| \|x_T - x^*\| + \frac{L}{2} \| x^* - x_T \|^2≤∥∇f(x∗)∥∥xT​−x∗∥+2L​∥x∗−xT​∥2﻿ (Substitute ∥xT−x∗∥\|x_T - x^*\|∥xT​−x∗∥﻿ into this) gets the answer.

    1. projection onto l1-ball
  1. Coordinate Descent
    1. PL inequality:

      f: minimun x*, for some μ\muμ﻿, 12∥∇f(x)∥2≥μ(f(x)−f(x∗)),∀x∈Rd\frac{1}{2} \| \nabla f(x) \|^2 \geq \mu (f(x) - f(x^*)), \forall x \in \mathbb{R}^d21​∥∇f(x)∥2≥μ(f(x)−f(x∗)),∀x∈Rd﻿.

      Strong convexity ⇒ PL inequality, sketch proof:

      f(x∗)≥f(x)+∇f(x)(x∗−x)+μ2∥x∗−x∥2f(x^*) \geq f(x) + \nabla f(x) (x^* - x) + \frac{\mu}{2} \| x^* - x \|^2f(x∗)≥f(x)+∇f(x)(x∗−x)+2μ​∥x∗−x∥2﻿

      ≥f(x)+min⁡x(∇f(x)(x∗−x)+μ2∥x∗−x∥2)\geq f(x) + \min_x(\nabla f(x) (x^* - x) + \frac{\mu}{2} \| x^* - x \|^2)≥f(x)+minx​(∇f(x)(x∗−x)+2μ​∥x∗−x∥2)﻿

      =f(x)−12μ∥∇f(x)∥2= f(x) - \frac{1}{2\mu} \| \nabla f(x) \|^2=f(x)−2μ1​∥∇f(x)∥2﻿

      choose step-size η=1L\eta = \frac{1}{L}η=L1​﻿, gradient descent with any initialized x0x_0x0​﻿ satisfies

      f(xT)−f(x∗)≤(1−μL)T(f(x0)−f(x∗))f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq (1 - \frac{\mu}{L})^T (f(x_0) - f(x^*))f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤(1−Lμ​)T(f(x0​)−f(x∗))﻿, f: L-smooth

      sketch proof:

      f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−12L∥∇f(xt)∥2f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2 f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−2L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2﻿

      ≤f(xt)−μL(f(xt)−f(x∗))\leq f(x_t) - \frac{\mu}{L} (f(x_t) - f(x^*))≤f(xt​)−Lμ​(f(xt​)−f(x∗))﻿

      ⇒ f(xT)−f(x∗)≤(1−μL)T(f(x0)−f(x∗))f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq (1 - \frac{\mu}{L})^T (f(x_0) - f(x^*))f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤(1−Lμ​)T(f(x0​)−f(x∗))﻿

    1. Coordinate-wise Smoothness:

      In each dimension i has a smoothness parameter LiL_iLi​﻿

      algo:

      xt+1=xt−γi∇if(xt)eix_{t+1} = x_{t} - \gamma_i \nabla_i f(x_t) e_ixt+1​=xt​−γi​∇i​f(xt​)ei​﻿, γi=1Li\gamma_i = \frac{1}{L_i}γi​=Li​1​﻿

      f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−12Li∥∇if(xt)∥2f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L_i} \| \nabla_i f(x_t) \|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−2Li​1​∥∇i​f(xt​)∥2﻿

      proof: coordinate L-smooth definition

      Randomized CGD:

      sample i at random, xt+1=xt−γi∇if(xt)eix_{t+1} = x_{t} - \gamma_i \nabla_i f(x_t) e_ixt+1​=xt​−γi​∇i​f(xt​)ei​﻿, γi=1Li\gamma_i = \frac{1}{L_i}γi​=Li​1​﻿

      Then:

      E[f(xT)−f(x∗)]≤(1−μdL)T(f(x0)−f(x∗))\mathbb{E}[f(x_T) - f(x^*)] \leq ( 1 - \frac{\mu}{dL})^T (f(x_0) - f(x^*))E[f(xT​)−f(x∗)]≤(1−dLμ​)T(f(x0​)−f(x∗))﻿

      proof:f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−12Li∥∇if(xt)∥2 f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L_i} \| \nabla_i f(x_t) \|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−2Li​1​∥∇i​f(xt​)∥2﻿, then E(f(xt+1))=f(xt)−12L∑i=1d1d∥∇if(xt)∥2=f(xt)−12Ld∥∇f(xt)∥2\mathbb{E(f(x_{t+1}))} = f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L}\sum_{i = 1}^{d} \frac{1}{d} \|\nabla_i f(x_t) \|^2 = f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2Ld} \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2E(f(xt+1​))=f(xt​)−2L1​∑i=1d​d1​∥∇i​f(xt​)∥2=f(xt​)−2Ld1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2﻿

      ≤f(xt)−μLd(f(xt)−f(x∗))\leq f(x_t) - \frac{\mu}{Ld} (f(x_t) - f(x^*))≤f(xt​)−Ldμ​(f(xt​)−f(x∗))﻿, substract f(x∗)f(x^*)f(x∗)﻿ on both sides reaches the answer.

      Importance Sampling:

      sample i with prob p=Li∑j=1dLj p = \frac{L_i}{\sum_{j = 1}^{d} L_j}p=∑j=1d​Lj​Li​​﻿, xt+1=xt−γi∇if(xt)eix_{t+1} = x_{t} - \gamma_i \nabla_i f(x_t) e_ixt+1​=xt​−γi​∇i​f(xt​)ei​﻿

      Average smooth: Lˉ=1d∑i=1dLi\bar{L} = \frac{1}{d}\sum_{i = 1}^{d} L_iLˉ=d1​∑i=1d​Li​﻿,

      then with IS,E[f(xT)−f(x∗)]≤(1−μdLˉ)T(f(x0)−f(x∗)) \mathbb{E}[f(x_T) - f(x^*)] \leq ( 1 - \frac{\mu}{d \bar{L}})^T (f(x_0) - f(x^*))E[f(xT​)−f(x∗)]≤(1−dLˉμ​)T(f(x0​)−f(x∗))﻿

      Steepest CD

      choose i=arg max⁡i∥∇i f(xt)∣i = \argmax_{i} \| \nabla_i\ f(x_t)|i=argmaxi​∥∇i​ f(xt​)∣﻿, xt+1=xt−γi∇if(xt)eix_{t+1} = x_{t} - \gamma_i \nabla_i f(x_t) e_ixt+1​=xt​−γi​∇i​f(xt​)ei​﻿, same settings,

      thenf(xT)−f(x∗)≤(1−μdL)T(f(x0)−f(x∗)) f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq ( 1 - \frac{\mu}{dL})^T (f(x_0) - f(x^*))f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤(1−dLμ​)T(f(x0​)−f(x∗))﻿

      Greedy Coordinate Descent

      choose i, but with xt+1=arg min⁡λf(xt+λei)x_{t+1} = \argmin_{\lambda} f(x_t +\lambda e_i)xt+1​=argminλ​f(xt​+λei​)﻿

      f(x)=g(x)+h(x)f(x) = g(x) + h(x)f(x)=g(x)+h(x)﻿ with h(x)=∑ihi(xi)h(x) = \sum_{i} h_i(x_i)h(x)=∑i​hi​(xi​)﻿, g, h_i convex, then x is a global minimum of f.

      f(y)=g(y)+h(y)≥g(x)+∇g(x)T(y−x)+h(x)+∑i=1d(hi(yi)−hi(xi))f(y) = g(y) + h(y) \geq g(x) + \nabla g(x)^T (y - x) + h(x) + \sum_{i = 1}^{d} (h_i(y_i) - h_i(x_i))f(y)=g(y)+h(y)≥g(x)+∇g(x)T(y−x)+h(x)+∑i=1d​(hi​(yi​)−hi​(xi​))﻿

      ≥f(x)\geq f(x) ≥f(x)﻿

  1. Nonconvex functions

    f twice differentiable, bounded Hessians ∥∇f(x)∥2≤L\| \nabla f(x) \|^ 2 \leq L∥∇f(x)∥2≤L﻿, then f is smooth with parameter L

    proof:

    1. ∥∇f(x)−∇f(y)∥≤L∥x−y∥\| \nabla f(x) - \nabla f(y) \| \leq L \| x - y\|∥∇f(x)−∇f(y)∥≤L∥x−y∥﻿, let h(t)=f(x+t(y−x)) h(t) = f(x + t(y - x))h(t)=f(x+t(y−x))﻿
    1. h’(t)=f’(x+t(y−x))(y−x)h’(t) = f’(x + t(y - x)) (y - x)h’(t)=f’(x+t(y−x))(y−x)﻿, we computef(y)−f(x)−∇f(x)T(y−x) f(y) - f(x) - \nabla f(x)^T (y - x)f(y)−f(x)−∇f(x)T(y−x)﻿

    f(y)−f(x)−∇f(x)T(y−x)=h(1)−h(0)−∇f(x)T(y−x)=∫01h′(t)dt−∇f(x)T(y−x)=∫01(∇f(x+t(y−x))T(y−x)−∇f(x)T(y−x))dt=∫01(∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T(y−x)dt≤∫01∥(∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T∥∥(y−x)∥dt≤∫01L∥t(y−x)∥∥(y−x)∥=L2∥y−x∥2f(y) - f(x) - \nabla f(x)^T (y - x) = h(1) - h(0) - \nabla f(x)^T (y - x) = \int_{0}^{1} h'(t)dt - \nabla f(x)^T (y - x) = \int_{0}^{1} ( \nabla f(x + t(y - x))^T (y - x) - \nabla f(x)^T (y - x) )dt = \int_{0}^{1} ( \nabla f(x + t(y - x)) - \nabla f(x))^T(y - x) dt \leq \int_{0}^{1}\| ( \nabla f(x + t(y - x)) - \nabla f(x))^T \| \| (y - x) \| dt \leq \int_{0}^{1} L\| t(y-x) \| \| (y - x) \| = \frac{L}{2} \| y - x\|^2f(y)−f(x)−∇f(x)T(y−x)=h(1)−h(0)−∇f(x)T(y−x)=∫01​h′(t)dt−∇f(x)T(y−x)=∫01​(∇f(x+t(y−x))T(y−x)−∇f(x)T(y−x))dt=∫01​(∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T(y−x)dt≤∫01​∥(∇f(x+t(y−x))−∇f(x))T∥∥(y−x)∥dt≤∫01​L∥t(y−x)∥∥(y−x)∥=2L​∥y−x∥2﻿

    f: global minimum x∗x^* x∗﻿, γ=1L\gamma = \frac{1}{L}γ=L1​﻿, GD yields:

    1T∑t=0T−1∥∇f(xt)∥2≤2LT(f(x0)−f(x∗))\frac{1}{T} \sum_{t = 0}^{T -1 } \| \nabla f(x_t) \|^2 \leq \frac{2L}{T} (f(x_0) - f(x^*))T1​∑t=0T−1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2≤T2L​(f(x0​)−f(x∗))﻿

    proof: sufficient decrease:f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−12L∥∇f(xt)∥2 f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \frac{1}{2L} \| \nabla f(x_t)\|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−2L1​∥∇f(xt​)∥2﻿ , rearrange and telescope

  1. Frank-Wolfe Algo

    constrained optimization: minimize f(x)f(x)f(x)﻿, x∈Xx \in Xx∈X﻿

    The algo: LMOx(g)=arg min⁡zgTzLMO_x (g) = \argmin_z g^TzLMOx​(g)=argminz​gTz﻿

    Frank-Wolfe: s=LMOx(∇f(xt))s = LMO_x (\nabla f(x_t))s=LMOx​(∇f(xt​))﻿, xt+1=(1−γt)xt+γtsx_{t+1} = (1 - \gamma_t) x_t + \gamma_t sxt+1​=(1−γt​)xt​+γt​s﻿

    Duality Gap: g(x)=∇f(x)T(x−s)g(x) = \nabla f(x)^T (x - s)g(x)=∇f(x)T(x−s)﻿, for s=LMOx(∇f(x)) s = LMO_x (\nabla f(x))s=LMOx​(∇f(x))﻿, then g(x)≥f(x)−f(x∗)g(x) \geq f(x) - f(x^*)g(x)≥f(x)−f(x∗)﻿. Sketch Proof:

    g(x)=∇f(x)T(x−s)≥∇f(x)T(x−x∗)≥f(x)−f(x∗)≥0g(x) = \nabla f(x)^T (x - s) \geq \nabla f(x)^T (x - x^*) \geq f(x) - f(x^*) \geq 0g(x)=∇f(x)T(x−s)≥∇f(x)T(x−x∗)≥f(x)−f(x∗)≥0﻿

    Convergence in O(1ϵ)O(\frac{1}{\epsilon})O(ϵ1​)﻿ steps: suppose f is L-smooth

    let γt=2t+2\gamma_t = \frac{2}{t+2}γt​=t+22​﻿, then FW algorithm yields: f(xT)−f(x∗)≤2Ldiam(X)2T+1,T≥1f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{2L diam(X)^2}{T+1}, T \geq 1f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤T+12Ldiam(X)2​,T≥1﻿

    sketch proof:

    1. let xt+1=xt+γt(s−xt)x_{t+1} = x_t + \gamma_t (s - x_t)xt+1​=xt​+γt​(s−xt​)﻿ with γt∈[0,1]\gamma_t \in [0, 1]γt​∈[0,1]﻿, then

      f(xt+1)≤f(xt)−γtg(xt)+γt2L2∥s−xt∥2 f(x_{t+1}) \leq f(x_t) - \gamma_t g(x_t) + \gamma_t^2 \frac{L}{2} \| s - x_t \|^2f(xt+1​)≤f(xt​)−γt​g(xt​)+γt2​2L​∥s−xt​∥2﻿

    write h(x)=f(x)−f(x∗)h(x) = f(x) - f(x^*)h(x)=f(x)−f(x∗)﻿, then h(xt+1)≤h(xt)−γth(xt)+γt2L2∥s−xt∥2≤(1−γt)h(xt)+γt2Ch(x_{t+1}) \leq h(x_t) - \gamma_t h(x_t) + \gamma_t^2 \frac{L}{2} \| s - x_t \|^2 \leq (1 - \gamma_t) h(x_t) + \gamma_t^2 Ch(xt+1​)≤h(xt​)−γt​h(xt​)+γt2​2L​∥s−xt​∥2≤(1−γt​)h(xt​)+γt2​C﻿, we need to pove that for γt=2t+2\gamma_t = \frac{2}{t+2} γt​=t+22​﻿, h(xt)≤4Ct+1h(x_t) \leq \frac{4C}{t+1}h(xt​)≤t+14C​﻿, which could be proved by induction.

    Curvature Constant

    C(f,X)=sup⁡x,s∈X,γ∈(0,1],y=(1−γ)x+γsC_{(f, X)} = \sup_{x, s \in X, \gamma \in (0, 1], y = (1 - \gamma)x + \gamma s}C(f,X)​=supx,s∈X,γ∈(0,1],y=(1−γ)x+γs​﻿ 1γ2(f(y)−f(x)−∇f(x)T(y−x))\frac{1}{\gamma^2} (f(y) - f(x) - \nabla f(x)^T (y - x))γ21​(f(y)−f(x)−∇f(x)T(y−x))﻿,

    then if C(f,X)C_{(f, X)}C(f,X)​﻿ is th curvature constant of f over X, then with stepsize γt=2t+2\gamma_t = \frac{2}{t+2}γt​=t+22​﻿, FW algorithm yields:

    f(xT)−f(x∗)≤4C(f,X)T+1f(x_T) - f(x^*) \leq \frac{4C_{(f, X)}}{T+1}f(xT​)−f(x∗)≤T+14C(f,X)​​﻿, use the definition of C(f,X)C_{(f, X)}C(f,X)​﻿ and plug in the definition of y, y-x, then use the same strategy above.

  1. Newton’s Methods

    xt+1=xt−f(xt)f′(xt)x_{t+1} = x_t - \frac{f(x_t)}{f'(x_t)}xt+1​=xt​−f′(xt​)f(xt​)​﻿

    xt+1=xt−f’(xt)f′′(xt)↔xt+1=xt−∇2f(xt)−1∇f(xt)x_{t+1} = x_t - \frac{f’(x_t)}{f''(x_t)} \leftrightarrow x_{t+1} = x_t - \nabla^2 f(x_t)^{-1}\nabla f(x_t)xt+1​=xt​−f′′(xt​)f’(xt​)​↔xt+1​=xt​−∇2f(xt​)−1∇f(xt​)﻿

  1. Quasi-Newton’s Methods

    Secant method: approximation of ∇f(xt)\nabla f(x_t)∇f(xt​)﻿, Hessians

    Greenstadt method: approximate the inverse of Hessians by adding an error term: EtE_tEt​﻿

    BFGS: Greenstadt with parameter M=H’=Ht−1M = H’ = H_t^{-1}M=H’=Ht−1​﻿

    L-BFGS: H’=H+E∗H’ = H + E^*H’=H+E∗﻿

    H’=(1−σyTyTσ)H(1−yσTyTσ)+σσTyTσH’ = (1 - \frac{\sigma y^T}{y^T \sigma})H (1 - \frac{y \sigma^T}{y^T \sigma}) + \frac{\sigma \sigma^T}{y^T \sigma}H’=(1−yTσσyT​)H(1−yTσyσT​)+yTσσσT​﻿
